summary: DHH announces a brief trip to San Francisco and proposes an informal meetup with local Rails developers and enthusiasts. He invites anyone interested to organize a gathering near his hotel. The post reflects the early grassroots community-building phase of Ruby on Rails in 2005, when the framework was still emerging. It's a simple, casual call to connect with the nascent Rails community in person.
key_points: DHH is visiting San Francisco for about five days and wants to meet local Rails developers
The casual tone reflects the small, tight-knit nature of the early Rails community in 2005
He asks the community itself to organize the meetup, suggesting a collaborative grassroots culture
money_quotes: It would be fun to meet up for a drink with the natives already doing or interested in Rails.
key_insight: In its earliest days, the Rails community was small enough that its creator could casually propose a drink with anyone interested in the framework.
